stages.name: Solid Rocket Boosters
stages.notes: 5-segment variant of Space Shuttle SRBs. Recovered and refurbished in Shuttle era; expended on SLS.

stages.name: Core Stage
stages.location: Michoud Assembly Facility, New Orleans, LA
stages.notes: Tallest rocket stage ever built. Core of the SLS stack. Primary systems engineering at Marshall Space Flight Center, Huntsville AL.
stages.msfc_role: PRIMARY — core stage systems engineering, propulsion, structures, cryogenic systems

stages.name: RS-25 Engines
stages.notes: Heritage Space Shuttle Main Engines with upgraded avionics. Among the most efficient rocket engines ever built. Expended on SLS.

stages.name: Interim Cryogenic Propulsion Stage
stages.notes: Modified Delta IV Cryogenic Second Stage. Boosts Orion from low Earth orbit to translunar injection.
stages.msfc_role: Upper stage integration and mission design

stages.name: Orion Stage Adapter
stages.notes: Structural interface between ICPS and Orion. Carried 13 CubeSat secondary payloads on Artemis I.

stages.name: Orion MPCV
stages.contractor: Lockheed Martin (crew module) / ESA (European Service Module)
stages.propellant: NTO / MMH hypergolic (service module main engine)
stages.notes: Multi-Purpose Crew Vehicle. Radiation-hardened avionics. 50% larger crew cabin than Apollo. Heat shield 5m diameter — largest ever flown.
stages.msfc_role: Mission operations coordination and payload integration
